reason: The statement applies because in the text fragment, King Hezekiah of Judah was forced to give a large amount of silver and gold to the king of Assyria, as well as cutting off gold from the doors of the temple of the LORD to give to the king of Assyria. Additionally, the king of Assyria sent a great host against Jerusalem, demanding surrender and tribute from Hezekiah. This demonstrates the Hebrews being forced to work for an evil king (Sennacherib) for the benefit of his kingdom (Assyria).
